what_it_does: Tests the fallback behavior of summarizeConversation when resuming a Claude SDK session fails or the recorded working directory is missing: it stubs the SDK query(), drives success/error/cwd scenarios, and asserts retry logic, error propagation, and option shaping.
observations: Uses vitest with vi.mock() to replace @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk query() before importing the summarizer.
Provides asyncIterableFor() to turn arrays into SDK-style async iterables of result objects.
Provides makeExchange() as a factory that spreads overrides over a default ConversationExchange.
Tests that a failed resume (is_error) triggers a second query() call without resume and with the conversation text inlined.
Tests that the recorded cwd is passed only when it still exists on disk, otherwise omitted.
Tests that non-resume SDK errors (thrown exceptions and non-resume subtypes like auth_failed) do not trigger the fallback.
Assumes the summarizer strips <summary>...</summary> XML wrappers to return plain text.
standouts: Resume-fallback retry removes options.resume and injects the conversation text into the prompt.
cwd is validated against disk existence before being forwarded to the SDK.
SummarizerSdkError preserves the SDK subtype and session_id.
conventions: Uses vi.mocked(query) for typed access to the mocked SDK function.
Uses a factory helper with a default object and ...overrides spread.
Cleans up the temporary directory in a try/finally block.
Casts async iterable returns with as any.
concerns: Heavy use of as any on mock return values weakens type safety.
Tests rely on undocumented tag-stripping behavior (<summary> wrapper).
Hardcoded /tmp paths in the default exchange may be platform-sensitive.
vi.mock placement before imports is correct but brittle if module graph changes.
notes: A focused contract test for summarizeConversation's SDK resume fallback, cwd validation, and error-classification behavior.
questions: What exact subtypes does isResumeFailure consider resume-related versus fatal?
How does summarizeConversation parse or strip the <summary> XML wrapper?
What fields does ConversationExchange require beyond those shown in the factory?
Does the SDK expect options.resume and options.cwd as shown, or are those internal conventions?
What happens if the SDK yields a mix of result and error chunks in one iterable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test Claude resume fallback and cwd-mismatch recovery, which matches the scenarios exercised.
apparent_themes: Test isolation via mocking and factory helpers
intention_signals: To guarantee that summarizeConversation can recover from stale or mismatched Claude SDK sessions by retrying with a fresh session and inlined context, while correctly surfacing SDK errors and avoiding retries for non-resume failures.
novelty: Combines SDK resume-session fallback with filesystem-based cwd validation and subtype-aware error classification in a single test suite.
contribution: A concrete, scenario-based specification of the summarizer's SDK contract: when and how to retry, how to handle missing cwd, and how to propagate SDK error metadata without over-generalizing retry.
summary_for_embedding: A vitest suite that stubs the Claude SDK query() to verify summarizeConversation's resume fallback, cwd existence handling, and SummarizerSdkError propagation.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the recoverability and error-surfacing contract of the conversation summarizer when SDK sessions fail or worktrees disappear.

symbols.does: Builds an AsyncIterable from an array so tests can simulate SDK query() return values.
symbols.description: This helper wraps a plain array of SDK message objects in an object implementing Symbol.asyncIterator, yielding each value asynchronously. It lets tests return arbitrary iterables from the mocked query() function without depending on the real SDK's streaming shape.

symbols.does: Constructs a ConversationExchange with sensible defaults and user-supplied overrides.
symbols.description: This factory returns a fully populated ConversationExchange object, spreading any overrides onto a fixed default. It centralizes test-data creation so each scenario can vary only the fields it cares about, such as cwd or message content.
